description: Zphyr evaluation harness dataset schema. Every JSONL line in datasets/ must conform to this schema.

id.description: Unique stable identifier. Format: '{category_prefix}-{NNN}', e.g. 'tech-042'. Never reuse or recycle IDs.

raw_asr_text.description: Verbatim ASR output — exactly what the recognizer produced, including fillers, repetitions, disfluencies.

literal_reference.description: What a perfect ASR would produce: clean transcription, no formatting, no filler removal. Used for L1a WER/CER evaluation independently of pipeline quality.

final_expected_text.description: What the full post-ASR pipeline should produce for the user. The primary correctness reference for L2 evaluation.

acceptable_variants.description: Structurally equivalent alternative outputs that are also accepted as correct. Avoids brittle byte-identity requirements while not being too permissive. Example: different list marker styles.

protected_terms.description: Exact spans that MUST appear unchanged in final_text. Checked case-sensitively, per occurrence. Rules: (a) every occurrence must survive, (b) overlapping terms: both checked independently, longest-match canonical, (c) one term substring of another: both checked separately.

extracted_command_expected.description: Expected command extraction result. null means NO command should be extracted — a spurious extraction is a hard failure.

rewrite_allowed_level.description: Controls semantic transformation tolerance. 'none': deterministic formatting only (filler removal, whitespace, spoken-punct substitution, capitalization rules) — any content-changing insertion or deletion is a hard failure. 'light': light structural normalization allowed, no paraphrase. 'full': LLM rewrite may run, integrity checks still apply.

context_type.description: Category used for per-category metric breakdowns and regression tracking.

app_type.description: Target application context. Affects formatting rules in some pipeline modes.

formatting_mode.description: Pipeline formatting mode to use when running this case. Matches FormattingMode in the app.

language.description: BCP-47 language code of the dictation. Examples: 'en', 'fr', 'zh', 'ru', 'es', 'ja'.

notes.description: Human-readable rationale explaining what this case tests, especially for edge cases and non-obvious expected outputs.

must_never_regress_subsets: All cases where protected_terms contains a URL or email
All cases where protected_terms contains an acronym (all-caps token)
